Given some categorized constants in the.response:
```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
clients("Aimee"; "Ginger"; "Freda"; "Hannah").
price(150; 160; 170; 180).
masseuse("Lynda"; "Nancy"; "Teri"; "Whitney").

% For each client, it matches with exactly 1 price and 1 masseuse.
{match(X, P, M): price(P), masseuse(M)}=1 :- clients(X).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
diplomats("Gilbert"; "Macdonald"; "Pickett"; "Vasquez").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
duration(4; 5; 9; 10).

% For each diplomat D, assign exactly 1 month M and 1 duration Dur.
{assign(D, M, Dur): months(M), duration(Dur)}=1 :- diplomats(D).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
names("Cornick"; "Dreadco"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Permias"; "Rubicorp"; "Techtrin"; "Ubersplore").

% Define the predicate launch(Name, Company, Month) to represent the launch of a name by a company in a given month.
% Each name is launched by exactly one company in exactly one month.
{launch(N, C, M): companies(C), months(M)}=1 :- names(N).

% Constraint: "Worul" is not launched in January (month 1) or February (month 2).
:- launch("Worul", _, 1).
:- launch("Worul", _, 2).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
witnesses("Benny Baron"; "Edith Estes"; "Hal Harrison"; "Iva Ingram").
dates(4; 5; 6; 7).
towns("Islesboro"; "Long Barn"; "Tarzana"; "Zearing").

% For each witness, match with exactly one date and one town.
{sighting(W, D, T): dates(D), towns(T)}=1 :- witnesses(W).

% Additional constraint: Hal Harrison's sighting must be after Iva Ingram's.
:- sighting("Hal Harrison", D1, _), sighting("Iva Ingram", D2, _), D1 <= D2.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
```
```asp
islands("Fushil"; "Jujihm"; "Nuhirk"; "Verinya").
year(1754; 1761; 1768; 1775).
culture("Hakili"; "Manika'i"; "Kukani"; "Wainani").

% The core relation: each island is discovered in a year and has a native culture.
discover(I, Y, C) :- islands(I), year(Y), culture(C).

% Each island, year, and culture is used exactly once.
{discover(I, Y, C)}=1 :- islands(I), year(Y), culture(C).
{discover(I, Y, C)}=1 :- islands(I), year(Y), culture(C).
{discover(I, Y, C)}=1 :- islands(I), year(Y), culture(C).

% Clue 1: Jujihm was discovered in 1768.
discover("Jujihm", 1768, _).

% Clue 2: Verinya was discovered in 1761.
discover("Verinya", 1761, _).

% Clue 3: Jujihm was discovered 14 years after the island on which the Wainani people lived.
% So Wainani island was discovered in 1754.
discover(_, 1754, "Wainani").

% Clue 4: The island on which the Kukani people lived was discovered 7 years after Fushil.
% So Fushil must be 1754 (since 1754+7=1761, and 1761 is taken by Verinya).
% Thus, Fushil → 1754, and Kukani → 1761 → Verinya.
discover("Fushil", 1754, _).
discover("Verinya", 1761, "Kukani").

% Clue 5: The island discovered in 1768 was either the island on which the Hakili people lived or Fushil.
% But Fushil is 1754, so it must be Hakili.
discover("Jujihm", 1768, "Hakili").
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
```
```asp
employee(1; 2; 3; 4).
riders(50; 75; 100; 125).
section("blue"; "green"; "red"; "yellow").

% Core predicate: assign each employee to a section and number of riders served.
assign(E, R, S) :- employee(E), riders(R), section(S).

% Ensure each employee is assigned to exactly one section and one rider count.
{assign(E, R, S) : riders(R), section(S)} = 1 :- employee(E).

% Ensure each section is assigned to exactly one employee and one rider count.
{assign(E, R, S) : employee(E), riders(R)} = 1 :- section(S).

% Ensure each rider count is assigned to exactly one employee and one section.
{assign(E, R, S) : employee(E), section(S)} = 1 :- riders(R).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.

games(8; 9; 10; 11).
positions("center field"; "first base"; "shortstop"; "third base").

% For each player P, they play exactly one game G and occupy exactly one position Pos.
{play(P, G, Pos): games(G), positions(Pos)}=1 :- players(P).
```
